title: Homemade Pizza

overview.description: A crisp, airy homemade pizza made with bread flour, simple tomato sauce and your choice of toppings.

method: Mix the flour, yeast and salt in a large bowl, keeping the salt and yeast separate until mixing begins.
Add the water and olive oil.
Knead for 10 minutes until smooth and elastic.
Cover and leave to rise until doubled in size, about 1–2 hours.
Meanwhile, mix the passata with the olive oil, garlic, oregano, salt and pepper.
Preheat the oven to its highest setting with a baking tray or pizza stone inside for at least 30 minutes.
Divide the dough into 4 equal portions.
Stretch each piece into a round about 30cm across without using a rolling pin if possible.
Spread over a thin layer of sauce.
Top with the mozzarella and your chosen toppings.
Bake for 8–12 minutes until the crust is golden and the cheese is bubbling.

learning.did-you-know: Most home ovens never reach the temperatures of traditional pizza ovens, so preheating the baking surface is one of the biggest improvements you can make.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How to make a versatile pizza dough, stretch it correctly and bake it successfully in a conventional oven.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: A family favourite that's cheaper, healthier and more customisable than takeaway pizza, while making good use of pantry staples.
chapman-tips: Less topping usually makes a better pizza—the dough should still be the star.
Tear mozzarella rather than slicing it so it melts more evenly.
Preheat the baking tray with the oven for the crispiest base.
A light dusting of semolina on the peel or baking paper helps the pizza slide.
notes.batch-cooking: Freeze individual dough balls after the first rise or freeze cooked pizza slices.
notes.leftovers: Reheat in a dry frying pan with a lid for a crisp base and melted cheese.
